evals.prompt: We're an NDC startup pursuing a TRL 4-6 cyber prototype OT under 10 USC 4022. The solicitation is in the workspace (DIU consortium-brokered, San Diego, ~18 months, target $2.4M). Build the milestone proposal structure AND our bid cost stack so I can see what to propose at each gate and what it should cost us to deliver.
evals.expected_output: A JSON envelope at artifacts/ot_prototype_bid.json with workflow='respond_to_ot_solicitation', authority='10 USC 4022', performer_type='ndc', cost_share_path='4022(d)(1)(A)' with required=false, milestones[] (3-5 entries with milestone_id, phase, trl_in/trl_out, est_duration_months, deliverables[], payment_type, exit_criterion, should_cost_mid), labor[] benchmarked from BLS for the SOC codes (cybersecurity engineer 15-1212 minimum) at the San Diego MSA with explicit aging factor, calc_plus[] anchors with N reported, materials[] sized to prototype-type heuristic (software/hybrid 10-40%), travel[] for any test-event trips with GSA per diem, consortium_fee block (5% default for DIU), totals.{government_obligation_low/mid/high, performer_share}, and a bid_recommendation block with target_price, rationale, and at least 3 risk_flags. Methodology cites 10 USC 4022, 10 USC 4022(d)(1)(A), 10 USC 4003.
expectations: The skill called kg_entities at least once requesting types including 'solicitation', 'requirement', 'deliverable', 'evaluation_factor', 'performance_standard', or 'place_of_performance'.
The skill called mcp__bls_oews__get_wage_data or mcp__bls_oews__igce_wage_benchmark for cybersecurity SOC 15-1212 (or 15-1252 software developer) at the San Diego metro.
The skill called mcp__gsa_calc__igce_benchmark or mcp__gsa_calc__price_reasonableness_check at least once.
The skill called mcp__gsa_perdiem__get_per_diem_by_zip or mcp__gsa_perdiem__estimate_travel_cost for the test-event travel line item (San Diego or test range).
The skill read references/ot_authority_taxonomy.md via read_file.
The skill read references/trl_milestone_patterns.md via read_file.
The skill read references/cost_models/ot_milestone_buildup.md via read_file.
The skill read references/relationship_query_patterns.md via read_file before calling kg_entities.
The skill called write_file with target path 'artifacts/ot_prototype_bid.json'.
The output authority field equals '10 USC 4022' (NOT '10 USC 4021' — this is a prototype, not research).
performer_type equals 'ndc' AND cost_share_path equals '4022(d)(1)(A)' AND cost_share_required equals false.
milestones array has at least 3 entries; every entry has a non-null exit_criterion (binary pass/fail); every payment_type is 'fixed' or 'cost-type' (not 'tbd').
Every labor entry has a buildup with non-null hourly rate AND the BLS series_id is reported.
consortium_fee.rate equals 0.05 (DIU default) and is added to government_obligation, not deducted from performer share.
bid_recommendation has a non-null target_price AND at least 3 risk_flags.
Methodology citations include both '10 USC 4022' AND '10 USC 4022(d)(1)(A)'.

evals.prompt: An AFRL research OT under 10 USC 4021 just dropped — TRL 2-4 autonomy algorithm work, university PI, Boston metro, 24 months, $1.8M. We're a traditional prime teaming with MIT. Build the project description framing AND the bid cost stack — but get the cost-share story right because the source doc shows confusing numbers.
evals.expected_output: A JSON envelope at artifacts/ot_research_bid.json with workflow='respond_to_ot_solicitation', authority='10 USC 4021', cost_share_required=false (4021 has NO statutory cost-share trigger), milestone structure mapped to research phases (typically 2-3 milestones across TRL 2-4), labor[] using academic burden scenarios (1.65/1.85/2.05 instead of 1.8/2.0/2.2) and grad_research_assistant entries at $55-75/hr institutional rate (NOT BLS-derived), postdoc entries at $80-110/hr, materials sized to research heuristic (low — 10-20%), and methodology that EXPLICITLY states '10 USC 4022(d) cost-sharing paths are statutorily inapplicable' to a 4021 research OT.
expectations: The skill called kg_entities for solicitation/requirement/place_of_performance.
The skill called mcp__bls_oews__get_wage_data for the Boston metro for at least one labor category.
The skill read references/ot_authority_taxonomy.md AND references/cost_models/ot_milestone_buildup.md via read_file.
The skill called write_file with target path 'artifacts/ot_research_bid.json'.
The output authority field equals '10 USC 4021' (NOT '10 USC 4022').
cost_share_required equals false AND methodology_notes contains the phrase 'statutorily inapplicable' or '4021' adjacent to 'no cost-share'.
At least one labor entry uses an academic burden scenario (low/mid/high in the 1.65/1.85/2.05 band, not 1.8/2.0/2.2).

At least one labor entry has role='graduate_research_assistant' OR role='grad_ra' with hourly rate between 55 and 75 (institutional rate, NOT BLS), AND methodology_notes flags the institutional-rate basis (not BLS-derived).
Methodology citations include '10 USC 4021' but do NOT cite '10 USC 4022(d)' as a binding authority on this agreement.

evals.prompt: Strategic question — we're a traditional prime (no NDC, no SB) with no teaming partner yet, looking at a 10 USC 4022 prototype OT. We can either bid solo (path C, 1/3 cost share required) or team with a small NDC sub. Run the cost-share scenario comparison so we know which path is cheaper out-of-pocket. Assume a $3.0M total project, traditional prime carries 80% of the work, NDC sub carries 20%.
evals.expected_output: A JSON envelope at artifacts/ot_strategy_compare.json with workflow='cost_share_path_compare', two scenarios labeled scenario_solo (path 4022(d)(1)(C), 1/3 cost share) and scenario_team (path 4022(d)(1)(A), NDC participation satisfies, no cost share if NDC participation is 'significant' = ≥33%), per-scenario government_obligation_mid and performer_out_of_pocket_mid, a participation_gate flag noting that 20% sub work-share is BELOW the typical 33% significant-participation threshold and that path A is at AO discretion, and a recommendation that increasing sub work-share to ≥33% OR adding additional NDC participation reduces our exposure by ~$1M at the standard 1/3 share. Methodology cites 10 USC 4022(d)(1)(A), 10 USC 4022(d)(1)(C), and 10 USC 3014 (NDC definition).
expectations: The skill called kg_entities for solicitation/requirement/place_of_performance/incumbent if present.
The skill read references/ot_authority_taxonomy.md via read_file.
The skill called write_file with target path 'artifacts/ot_strategy_compare.json'.
The output workflow field equals 'cost_share_path_compare'.
The output has TWO labeled scenarios: scenario_solo (or path_c) AND scenario_team (or path_a).
scenario_solo.cost_share_path equals '4022(d)(1)(C)' AND scenario_solo.performer_share_pct equals approximately 0.333 (within 0.01 tolerance).
scenario_team.cost_share_path equals '4022(d)(1)(A)' AND scenario_team.performer_share_pct equals 0.0.

The output contains a participation_gate flag noting that 20% sub work-share is below the 33% significant-participation threshold OR that 4022(d)(1)(A) significant-participation is an AO judgment without a statutory percentage.
Methodology citations include '10 USC 4022(d)(1)(A)' AND '10 USC 4022(d)(1)(C)' AND '10 USC 3014'.

evals.prompt: We're an NDC startup pursuing a TRL 4-6 cyber prototype OT under 10 USC 4022. The solicitation is in the workspace. Build the milestone proposal structure AND our bid cost stack, then return the standard narrative summary.
evals.expected_output: Narrative summary covering the 7 numbered points in the SKILL.md (workflow chosen, authority + performer + cost-share path, milestone structure, totals, top 3 risk flags, hand-off, statutory citations), backed by an actually-written JSON artifact on disk. Cover-note prose may use bold-bullet stats, milestone field readouts (Deliverables:, Exit criterion:, Traces to:, TRL in/out:), structured-field disclosure of artifact keys, and Class B reasoning markers (default, standard per, per AO convention, per taxonomy reference) — substantive claims live in the JSON envelope's source_chunks fields.
expectations: The skill MUST call write_file with a target path matching artifacts/ot_*.json before drafting the narrative summary (per SKILL.md v0.2.0 'REQUIRED — DO NOT SKIP' directive).

tools/audit_skill_grounding.py --skill ot-prototype-strategist reports grounding_ratio is >= 0.85 (milestone-and-cost-stack reasoning skill; the JSON artifact's source_chunks fields are the citation-of-record, the narrative is the human-readable distillation; 147.10 calibration credits cost-stack labor/travel bullets, BLS vintage caveats, Government-obligation envelope readouts, and capability-pointer offers as exempt).

tools/audit_skill_grounding.py reports claims_exempt is at least 0.40 of claims_total (most cover-note prose should be recognized as structural / milestone-bullet / structured-field / reasoning-leap, not unsourced claims).
No regression below 0.30 across at least three consecutive runs of the same prompt.
